commands.review_summary:
The PR adds stop-before-manual-install EACCES recovery guidance to the update CLI and Updating docs, with focused assertions for the hint text.

Reproducibility: yes. at the source-path level: current main can produce an EACCES global install failure and its hint omits the stop-before-manual-recovery instruction. I did not recreate the destructive live system-global npm replacement window during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No automated repair is indicated; the remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof or an explicit maintainer proof override.

target.title: fix(update): guide EACCES manual recovery
target.body:
## Summary

- add EACCES recovery hints that tell managed-Gateway operators to stop the Gateway before sudo/manual package recovery
- document the root-owned Linux system-global recovery path: stop Gateway, run the system npm install, refresh the Gateway service, restart, then verify
- add focused coverage for staged/global install EACCES hint text

## Why

Current main can restart the old managed Gateway after a staged package update fails with EACCES. The existing recovery hint points operators toward sudo/manual package recovery, but it does not say to stop the Gateway first. That leaves a window where the running Gateway can try to load core/plugin files while npm is replacing the package tree.

This follows ClawSweeper's narrow guidance on #83747: improve recovery hints/docs and focused tests without changing the updater lifecycle policy.

Closes #83747

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** EACCES recovery guidance for root-owned/system-global npm installs now fails closed at the operator level by instructing users to stop the managed Gateway before manual package replacement.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows desktop source checkout from upstream main `424c6d0a5`, Node `v24.13.0`, pnpm `11.1.0`. The issue itself includes live Ubuntu VPS evidence for the original half-swapped package state.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `codex-review` closeout
- **Evidence after fix:** `progress.test.ts` passed with 6 tests; `git diff --check` produced no output; final `codex-review` reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- **Observed result after fix:** EACCES hints include both user-writable-prefix recovery and a managed-Gateway system-install outline that stops the Gateway before package replacement and refreshes/restarts it afterward. Updating docs now include the same safe recovery sequence and verification commands.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a destructive live system-global upgrade/recovery on the VPS; this PR intentionally changes guidance/tests only and does not alter package-manager lifecycle behavior.

## Validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       6 passed (6)
```

```text
git diff --check
# no output
```

```text
codex-review
codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```
target.author: brokemac79
